canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, CUB-200-2011 (also referred to as CUB, CUB-200, or Caltech-UCSD Birds-200-2011) is consistently defined as a fine-grained image dataset containing 200 species of birds. Its most prevalent application is to measure model performance on fine-grained visual classification (FGVC) or broader image classification tasks. Several papers situate it within the FGVC benchmark suite, where it is used alongside other specialized datasets. The dataset is used to evaluate a range of behaviors, including zero-shot classification, prediction correctness, and the performance of concept-based models. While the dominant use is for classification, a smaller line of work applies the dataset to different tasks. For instance, one paper uses it to evaluate models on image captioning, image generation, image reconstruction, and cross-modal retrieval. Thus, its primary role in the surveyed literature is as a benchmark for fine-grained visual recognition, with less frequent applications in generative and retrieval contexts.
papers: Faithful Vision-Language Interpretation via Concept Bottleneck Models
NOLA: Compressing LoRA using Linear Combination of Random Basis
Analyzing and Boosting the Power of Fine-Grained Visual Recognition for Multi-modal Large Language Models
Enhancing Cognition and Explainability of Multimodal Foundation Models with Self-Synthesized Data
Revisiting Prefix-tuning: Statistical Benefits of Reparameterization among Prompts
Portable Reward Tuning: Towards Reusable Fine-Tuning across Different Pretrained Models
WeGeFT: Weight-Generative Fine-Tuning for Multi-Faceted Efficient Adaptation of Large Models
Beyond Accuracy: Ensuring Correct Predictions With Correct Rationales

definitions.text: The Caltech-UCSD Birds-200-2011 dataset, a fine-grained image dataset with 200 bird species, used for tasks like image captioning and generation.
The Caltech-UCSD Birds-200-2011 image classification benchmark used for evaluating prediction correctness.
